behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: history management must feed the LLM request builder correctly. A unit test of a trim helper can pass while orchestration forgets to apply trimming before the LLM call. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and no model-output quality is under test, so Integration is the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Wire chat handler + prompt builder + fake LLM client that records request payload. Send a 20-message history, execute one response cycle, and assert the payload contains exactly the most recent 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment/output quality (relevance, specificity, topicality). Deterministic stubs would hide this failure by construction. Rejected Workflow: behavior text describes one generation quality check, not a required multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the production prompt path against the real LLM on a labeled set of customer questions/history pairs. Score whether each response answers the asked question (not generic/off-topic) using rubric-based evaluation, and fail if relevance rate drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires sequential agentic steps: initial LLM draft, tone judgment, then regeneration decision path. The core failure is in chained model-involved handoff, not a single isolated call. Rejected Agentic: phrase "identifies ... and triggers regeneration" requires multi-step sequencing, not just one model output check.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with real model calls enabled for draft and tone-check/regeneration path on casual-tier cases designed to elicit formal first drafts. Assert the system flags over-formal tone and performs regeneration in-sequence.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at local module boundaries (orchestrator -> persistence/repository -> timestamp fields). A unit test of any single method misses cross-module record completeness and ordering. Rejected System: behavior text only requires DB persistence correctness, which is catchable with in-process/local test DB wiring; no real network/OS primitive is necessary. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: assertions are about persistence integrity, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run chat flow with deterministic stubbed model outputs including a forced regeneration path, writing to a real local test DB. Assert stored records include user message, first draft, regenerated draft, and valid timestamps (present, correctly ordered, linked to same conversation).

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestration/state handoff from regeneration logic to customer-facing output channel. This is best caught by wiring response pipeline modules together. Rejected Agentic using behavior text: "When the tone check triggers regeneration" conditions on trigger already occurring, so this test need not validate model decision quality; it validates replacement/visibility logic. Rejected Workflow for same reason: no requirement to validate multiple model judgments, only post-trigger output selection.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate handler + tone-check gate + response emitter with deterministic stubs, force regeneration path, and assert only regenerated text is emitted/returned to customer while rejected draft is never sent.
